content.full_text:
SACRAMENTO
770 L STREET, SUITE 8OO
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (976',) 446-6752
LAW OFFICES OF
NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
PARRINELLO, MUELLER & NAYLOR
A PARTNERSHIP TNCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, 
'4OOO
MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 9{941
TELEPHONE (41s) 389-6800
SAN FRANCISCO
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800
July 8, L992
) ig92
Ms. Linda Cassidy
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Analysis & Assistance
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 
9581-2-0807
Re: Tosco Refining Company's Third Quarter l-991-
Lobbyist Employer Report
Dear Linda:
This letter requests written confirmation of advice you
provided to me in person last week and over the telephone earlier
this week. (Gov. Code S 831L6 and California Code Regs.
S 18239.) We have provided you with all facts material to
answering our guestion, and would therefore appreciate receiving
formal advice. We are the authorized representative of Tosco
Refining Company and reguest this advice on its behalf.
Facts
Tosco recently hosted a large dinner and dancing event to
reward employees for their hard work on a particular company
project. 
The event cost approximately $30,000i the cost per
perlon was $70. (This figure includes dinner, drinks, a band and
otner entertainment, hotel rental, transportation costs, etc.)
An employee invited two state officials to the event, one of whom
reprelenls the district 
where the company is located. The event
wouta have been held had they not attended. No arrangements for
the event were rnodified in consideration of their expected
attendance.
On its Form 635 filed for that guarter, Tosco disclosed that
it had made activity expenses to each of the state officials in
the amount of $70, ior a total activity expense of $l-40. The
Secretary of State sent back the Forn 635, indicating that the
company had not properly disclosed the total costs of the
activily expense event. (See May 12 L992, letter enclosed).


'i 
\
t. )l
,l
I
I
I
I


Ms. Linda Cassidy
JuIy 8, 1992
Page 2
AiaIvsl, a
Lobbyist employers Dust report any payBents they make which
benefit state officials, along with the total cost of any event
at which the legislators receive such palrments. (cov. code
S 86116.) In other words, if a company pays for dinner for an
enployee (who is not a registered lobbyist) and two state
officials at $75 total and $25 per person, the lobbyist employer
must report both that the state officials received $25 acti.rity
expenses and that the total cost of the event was $75. In order
to courplete the sunmary section of Forn 635, the iobbyist
employer nust list the total costs of all such events.
You indicated that Tosco need not list $30,000 as the total
cost of the event, because the event itself rras not related to
the company's lobbying interests. Instead, the conpany invited
state officials to, and made activity expenses at, an event
unrelated to any legislative or political goa1s. In this case,
you told me that the |ttotal cost of the activity expense eventrr
was $140 ($70 times 2). Given the event size and the fact that
the company bore the cost for a nonlegislative reason, we alrree
with your conclusion and unless told othemise by you will advise
Tosco not to amend their Forn 635. Your conclusion is identical
to the instructions provided in FPPC Lobbyinq NewsLetter,
vol . 12, no. 1 (Jan. 1990) at p. 7 (copy enclosed).
Please call me irnrnediately if this letter does not
accurately reflect your advice.
s incerely,
k#
JRS/sfr
Enclosures
cc: Barbara Snith, secretary of State's office
Ann farner, Tosco
JiIl Brurnleve, Tosco
5714. 01

sections.question: Whether Tosco Refining Company must report the total cost of a $30,000 employee reward dinner event ($70 per person) on its Form 635 Lobbyist Employer Report when two state officials were invited and received $70 in activity expenses each, or whether only the $140 total activity expense ($70 × 2) should be reported since the event was unrelated to the company's lobbying interests.

sections.question_synthetic: Must a lobbyist employer report the full cost of an employee appreciation event on Form 635 when state officials attend, or only the per-person cost multiplied by the number of officials attending, when the event is unrelated to lobbying activities?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney seeking formal written confirmation of prior informal FPPC advice. The letter describes facts, references prior advice, and requests confirmation but does not contain a separate 'Question' or 'Conclusion' section with verbatim formal responses. The attorney summarizes the FPPC's prior informal position within the body of the letter.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Tosco Refining Company must report the total cost of a $30,000 employee reward dinner event ($70 per person) on its Form 635 Lobbyist Employer Report when two state officials were invited and received $70 in activity expenses each, or whether only the $140 total activity expense ($70 × 2) should be reported since the event was unrelated to the company's lobbying interests.

embedding.summary: A law firm requests written confirmation from FPPC of prior informal advice regarding whether Tosco Refining Company should report the total $30,000 cost of an employee dinner event on its Form 635, or only the $140 in activity expenses provided to two attending state officials, given the event was unrelated to lobbying.
